folio.label: 27v
folio.l: But Penneus dougúter , whicú æat higút Dane
There saugú I Atheou~ , an hert I maked
For vengeaunce æ he saugú , Dane al naked
I saw how æ hise houndes , han hi~ caugút

note.notetxt: 1209 F.27v there is a change of ink roughly at this line, from lighter brown to brown. The lighter brown started at folio 26r.ODR

folio.l: And fretyn hi~ , for æ æei knew hi~ naugút
ñet peynted was , a litel forthermore
How Atthalaunce , honted the wylde bore
And Meleagre , and many an other moo
For whicú , Diane , wrou©t he~ care and woo
There saw I many a noæä , wonder story
The whicú me list nat drawe , to memory
This goddesse on an hert , ful heye sette
With smale houndes , al a boute hire fete
And vndirnethe hire feet , she had a Mone
Waxyng it was , & shulde vanysshe sone
In gaude grene , hire stature clothed was
With bow in hond , and arwes in a cas
Hire eyen cast she , ful lowe a doun
There Pluto hath , his derke regiou~
A wo~man tuaillyng , was hire biforn~
But for hire child , so longe was vn born~
Ful pytously lucyna , gan she calle
And seide help , for æè maist best of alle
Wel coude he peynt lifly , that it wrougút
With many a floreyn , he the hewes bougút
¢ Now ben theise lystes mad , and Theseus

note.notetxt: 1231there is a cross in dry point in the margin.ODR

folio.l: That at his gret cost , arrayed thus
The temples , and the teatre euäy dele
Whan it was don , hi~ liked wonder wele
But stynte I wyl , of Theseus a lyte
And speke of Palamou~ , and of Arcite
¢ The day approcheth , of here returnynge
That euäych shulde , an , C , knygútis brynge
The bataylle to darreyne , as I ©ow told
And to Athenes , here couennt for to hold
Hath euäycú of hem , brougút an , C , knygútes
Wel armed for the werre , at alle rigútes
And sekerly , there trowed many a man
That neuäe sithen , æ the world bygan
As for to speke , of knyghthod of here hond
As fer as god hath maked , see and lond
Nas of so fewe , so noble a companye
For euäych wygút , æ loued cheualrye
And wold his thankes , han a passau~t name
